other_observers: None.; Many reports beginning in December of probable HOREs at the Discovery Center.
description: Views through a high-powered, high-quality scope of bird in good light in a tree to the west of the Discovery Museum feeders.  A colder looking, more black/white/gray redpoll lacking the warm tones of the nearby COREs.   Snowy-white undertail coverts lacking any streaks.   Flanks had faint/pale gray streaks.   Head: red cap was small surrounded by frosty gray with very little black on the face (no goatee).   Breast had a pale pink wash which was only on the upper breast and didn't extend onto the throat or the belly.   Unfortunately the bird failed too cooperate and provide views of its rumpBehaviors: Nothing notable. Was sitting on a branch catching some sun.Call: None detectedPlumage: basic
